fresh_facts.claim: US and Israeli forces conducted airstrikes on Iranian oil infrastructure at Kharg Island, bridges, railways, and IRGC headquarters on April 7-8, 2026.
fresh_facts.source: US Central Command / Netanyahu Defense Ministry

fresh_facts.claim: President Trump issued a final deadline on April 7, 2026 at 8 p.m. ET threatening attacks on Iran's energy infrastructure, bridges, and power plants if the Strait of Hormuz remains closed.
US Central Command reported over 13,000 targets damaged or destroyed across Iran during overnight airstrikes on April 7-8, 2026.
Israeli warplanes struck eight bridge segments in Tehran, Karaj, Tabriz, Kashan, and Qom used for Iranian weapons transport, confirmed by Netanyahu and Defense Minister Katz.
Iran launched at least seven repeated missile barrages at Israel on April 7, 2026, affecting southern Israel, Tel Aviv, central areas, and the West Bank.
Iran fired an explosive drone that struck a home in Zargazawi village, Erbil province, Iraq on early April 7, 2026, killing two civilians.
Iran's UN envoy Amir-Saeid Iravani vowed 'immediate and proportionate' retaliation if Trump acts on threats, calling them potential 'war crimes' and 'genocide'.
Israel intercepted multiple Iranian missile launches, including two just before sunset Tuesday April 7, 2026, targeting Tel Aviv and West Bank.
Hezbollah fired on northern Israel amid broader military exchanges on April 7, 2026.
Strikes near Bushehr nuclear plant prompted WHO evacuation of workers and health risk warnings on April 7, 2026.
Pakistan's PM urged Trump for a 2-week deadline extension and Iranian Strait reopening for ceasefire and diplomacy on April 7, 2026.
Russia and China vetoed a UN resolution to reopen the Strait of Hormuz on April 7, 2026 in an 11-2 vote.
President Masoud Pezeshkian faces fierce hardline backlash, with accusations of treason against former officials like Mohammad Javad Zarif and Hassan Rouhani over calls to conditionally end the war.
The IRGC pressured President Pezeshkian to appoint hardliner Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr to the Supreme National Security Council.
Tensions between President Pezeshkian and IRGC commander Ahmad Vahidi have led to a 'complete political deadlock' with the IRGC assuming control over state functions.
The assassination of Ali Larijani has weakened coordination within Iranian leadership.
Mojtaba Khamenei's rise aligns with the Iranian system's hardline design.
Iran's economy shows structural collapse with high inflation signaling a loss of faith in the currency amid war damages.
Iranian officials have framed Strait of Hormuz control as a financial asset to offset war costs.
The Strait of Hormuz closure is causing frequent and violent economic shocks with lingering oil and gas price impacts.
Iran's state broadcaster has escalated anti-dissent rhetoric, framing protests as enemy warfare and warning against revealing officials' locations.
Recent protests in Iran represent the most serious unrest the regime has faced, threatening its character.
Trump postponed planned US strikes on Iran's power grid on April 7, 2026 after claiming 'constructive' backchannel talks with Iran.
Iranian officials denied having direct contact with the Trump administration regarding the backchannel talks claim.
Saudi and Emirati leaders urged Trump to negotiate with Iran, but he prioritized Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's influence.
Iran, the US, and Israel all believe they are winning with no mutual desire to end the conflict as of April 7, 2026.

regime_fall.current_status: Day 42 escalation has reached an inflection point with US and Israeli forces conducting the largest coordinated airstrike campaign against Iran (13,000+ targets including Kharg Island, bridges, IRGC HQ, and nuclear facilities), while Trump's contradictory diplomatic posturing (5-day pause vs. final deadline threats) collides with Iranian denials of negotiations, hardline consolidation under Mojtaba Khamenei/Zolghadr, and growing internal political deadlock between President Pezeshkian and the IRGC.
regime_fall.last_major_event: US and Israeli forces destroyed over 13,000 targets across Iran on April 7-8, 2026, including Kharg Island oil infrastructure, eight strategic bridge segments, IRGC headquarters, and facilities near Bushehr nuclear plant, marking the most intensive phase of the 42-day escalation.
